reasoning: The question asks if there will be more than 10x the reference baseline of protests in Lebanon in the 30 days before Mar 22, 2026. The baseline given is 34-35 a month. This means we'd need over 350 protests in a single 30-day window (roughly 11.7 a day). Extensive searches covering February and March 2026 show massive war and escalation in Lebanon since late February 2026, leading to massive displacement and IDF operations. While there were some scattered protests in mid-February (VAT/fuel protests on Feb 18), large-scale continuous protest movements that generate >10 protests daily for a month are entirely absent from the news. Furthermore, wartime conditions and widespread evacuations typically heavily suppress normal civil society protests. 10x month-over-month spikes in event frequencies are historically incredibly rare without massive defining international coverage. Given no evidence of a nationwide coordinated uprising and the outbreak of heavy war, it is near-certain the threshold won't be met.
